description: Constitution Steward policy. Defines protected constitutional rules, amendment classes, required review roles, and human-required boundaries. Agents may propose policy changes but may not self-approve high-risk or constitutional changes.

boundaries.name: secrets-rotation
boundaries.description: Deleting or rotating secrets, tokens, or credentials

boundaries.name: db-schema-migration
boundaries.description: Changing Prisma schema or running migrations

boundaries.name: force-push-protected
boundaries.description: Force-pushing to main or protected branches

boundaries.name: control-plane-scripts
boundaries.description: Changing the launch gate, health gate, or batch launcher scripts

protectedConstitutionalRules.title: Explicit Merge Allowlists
rules: No implicit broadening — a worker assigned src/auth/** may not also fix a typo in src/posts/**
No self-granted extensions — a worker MUST NOT edit its task JSON or control-plane metadata to widen allowedFiles
Shared locks are explicit — access to shared resources requires a declared sharedLocks entry
Docs-only tasks stay in docs — a task whose allowedFiles are exclusively under docs/ MUST NOT edit source, scripts, or config

protectedConstitutionalRules.title: Main-Red Launch Stop
rules: No new feature or runtime workers may launch when main is red
Recovery takes priority over all other work
No override — the red-state block is absolute

protectedConstitutionalRules.title: Legacy Backend Read-Only Policy
rules: Workers MAY read legacy code to understand patterns or migration targets
Workers MUST NOT modify, refactor, or delete legacy files
Migration work requires a dedicated migration task with explicit human approval
Only the migration matrix updater may propose legacy file changes, and those require human review

protectedConstitutionalRules.title: No Worker Scope Expansion
rules: No self-expansion — a worker MUST NOT modify its task JSON, allowedFiles, conflictGroup, or sharedLocks after launch
No transitive expansion — discovering a dependency outside allowedFiles is a blocker, not an invitation
No orchestration self-promotion — a worker MUST NOT spawn sub-workers, create new tasks, or modify orchestrator scheduling state
No policy modification — a worker MUST NOT edit .github/ai-policy/, .github/ai-state/, or docs/ai-native/ unless in allowedFiles

constitutional.label: Constitutional Amendment
constitutional.scope: Changes to seed-constitution.md or this constitution-steward-policy.json
process: Corresponding update to docs mirror if applicable

policy-adjustment.label: Policy Adjustment
policy-adjustment.scope: Changes to .github/ai-policy/** files other than constitution-steward-policy.json and seed-constitution.md

operational-tuning.label: Operational Tuning
operational-tuning.scope: Changes to numeric thresholds, timeouts, or non-rule parameters within existing policies
process: PR with rationale for parameter change

constitution-auditor.label: Constitution Auditor
constitution-auditor.scope: Reviews all changes to seed-constitution.md, constitution-steward-policy.json, and high-risk policy files

control-plane-reviewer.label: Control Plane Reviewer
control-plane-reviewer.scope: Reviews changes to launch gate, health gate, batch launcher, and orchestration scripts

repo-owner.label: Repository Owner
repo-owner.scope: Final approval authority for constitutional amendments

humanRequiredBoundaries.description: Operations that MUST NOT be performed by any AI worker or automation without explicit human approval
humanRequiredBoundaries.enforcement: Workers encountering a task that touches these boundaries MUST comment on the issue requesting human intervention
humanRequiredBoundaries.note: Workers MUST NOT proceed even if allowedFiles technically includes the path
